Dr. Allen may not be the best professor, but she is definitely fair and equips students with the knowledge to do well. The students typing these reviews were probably asleep during lecture.

If you enjoy learning everything but whats on your exam, this is the class for you. Skip or go to class, doesn't matter you'll get the same grade. Some people sign up for a GPA boost, but if thats not you then this is your class.

I went to her lectures for the first 2 weeks and learned nothing. She goes in-depth with all the easy topics and skips the hard ones. Going to lecture does nothing to prepare you for the exam. If you want to teach this class to yourself, take Heather Allen. If you have to take her, read the textbook and watch Dr. Fus videos.

Dr. Allen does not know how to teach this class. She told us during the first lecture she hasn't taught Chem 1220 in a while, and that is very clear. Grading was inconsistent, but there are multiple chances to get it right.

Although Dr. Borland is a very nice guy and not a bad professor, I would never take his class again. The grading system used is unnecessarily harder and more complicated than it needs to be. If you have AP credit for this course I highly suggest you take it as it is easy to miss 6/33 standards and fail the class even with 100% on everything else.

Professor Borland is an amazing professor, but the grading system he uses is absolutely awful. If you are not used to a standards and applications grading system, do not take this class. That being said, he is very much so available and really does his hardest to help. I just think I personally do better in a smaller, more traditional class setting

Dr. Borland is a great professor, quick to respond to emails. I am taking the class at my high school and he has been very communicative when problems occurred. He also gets off track very easily during out of class hours, so just try to keep him on track.

analysis.name: Parisa Fateheddin
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She is a terrible teacher. I had her for my Calc 2 recitation and she talks the whole time without engaging the class. She is very confusing and dismissive of questions. Do not take this at all tasks. Her handwriting is too fast and messy. Take anything else.
Had her for 1152 rec, take anyone else! She is so hard to hear and sort of just rambles the whole class. She is also super unfriendly when you get questions wrong, etc.

Absolutely does not deserve the low rating she has. She always chooses helpful topics, reviews past content, and shows useful example problems. She also definitely cares about students; she had learned everyone's name by week 3 (!), is always happy to answer questions, and has a sense of humor. However, she does work through problems very speedily!

Parisa recitations can be boring, but they were so helpful for understanding the content. Might be controversial, but Ioved how she would cold call on students. It made pay attention the entire time. Parisa would also send out extra videos, and all of the notes after class which I really appreciated. Super nice!

Had her for calc 2 recitation, not good at teaching and reviewing concepts. Also has handwriting that cannot be read easily. Gives incorrect information for questions on written homework and exams. Seems nice but would not recommend as a teacher.

analysis.name: Clair  Farris
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 77 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Lectures were very useful. If you pay attention to them and do the work, it's not a hard class. People might say it's a lot of homework, but, again, it's relatively easy stuff. MS Access portion is a lot easier than the Excel portion; labs walk you through everything. Exams are all multiple-choice. Clair made everything a lot easier to understand.

The class itself is not difficult in any way. I didn't read any of the course notes or pay attention to a single lecture after the second week of school. As long as you work your way through the prelabs and inlabs and get the first-hand experience of working on them, then you will be just fine. Clair is an extremely nice person as well which helps.

I haven't finished the semester but I have almost a 99%! It's SO EASY to get a good grade if you just do the pre-labs and labs. Quizzes are super easy but you will have one each week. Honestly I didn't pay too much attention in the lectures and I was still able to get a good grade anyway. She is super kind if you go to her office hours and get help

Honestly, lecture isn't even necessary. If you read the course notes provided weekly there's really no point of showing up, as they explain everything in the lecture and then some. As long as you complete the labs, read the course notes, and do the weekly quiz, this class is an easy A and the exams really are not that challenging either.

Went to lecture for the first few weeks of the semester but had to stop simply because I could not stand the sound of her voice, just listening to her gave me a headache. You do NOT have to go to lecture at all during this course, as everything you will need is in the lecture notes. She just reads off lecture notes and adds nothing.

analysis.name: Rui Fang
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Honestly one of the kindest souls you will ever meet. Though it is hard to understand her lecture sometimes, she makes up for it through her office hours. Gives 2% extra credit. She records all lectures and posts all notes. Responds to emails within a couple hours. Loved her a lot and if all profs had her passion the world would be a better place.

Not very clear explanations of material. I relied on online resources a lot. Accent isn't the easiest to understand. Doesn't help that she often would have students show work on her iPad despite never showing the work herself, so it's hard to see what the correct way to do things is. Tests were often difficult but similar to the practice tests.

Dr. Fang is really nice. She provides lots of practice exams and quizzes beforehand so that you have expectations in mind. She is willing to help during office hours and replies to emails pretty fast. The lectures could have been done better, her accent could be confusing at times. If you are really to self-taught her class would be a good choice.

She is a very kind person, but not for a big lecture hall full of people. She sometimes made people uncomfortable by calling them up to do problems on her iPad in front of the whole class. Her office hour was helpful, though. She would claim that the test was "easy" when, in reality, it wasn't. The quizzes are weighted too much.

It was tough to understand her throughout the entire semester. Much of it was very theoretical and hard to visualize. I relied on teaching myself throughout the semester using Pearson, ChatGPT, and Khan. She loves calling up people to solve problems, and would pick randomly if no one raised their hands. I've heard better things about other profs.

analysis.name: Jack Fernandes
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I took PS4123 as a Session 1 class and although the turnaround time was short, he was a great professor that made lecture engaging and is very fair with his grading. You get what you put into the class and it's pretty easy to get an A, but he makes sure you learn the material too and encourages active participation (but not in a scary way)

Overall, it was a pretty easy class. I took it in the first session, so a lot was thrown at me quickly. But overall, very manageable class, there's just a lot of work in the last 2 weeks, but he's pretty lax on the grading.

Professor Fernandes was great! I took this as a full-session class, there were three exams overall, all open-note and very fair. The assignments and content were also enjoyable. It was definitely very chill and an easy A!
Really nice professor with very chill grading criteria. He really wants students to do well and will help you get there. Interesting topic as well, no complaints.
Really good professor! You can tell he really cares about his students and the material that he teaches.

analysis.name: Feride Tiglay
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 16 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: She's a very easy grader. If you miss class, she will give you credit if you finish the work. She's super sweet and very understanding if you miss class. She is also always willing to help you before or after class.

Professor Tiglay is not a bad professor. She is caring and always there to help. She even gives you partial credit on exams if you show work, even if you get the question wrong. However, she makes the grading system very test heavy which could negatively affect your grade pretty strongly. There is also a lot of videos for the lectures. It's okay.

She was very helpful when I actually attended class. Now, at the end of the semester, I didn't go to class for the entire last month and still finished with an A. I did miss points because of attendance, but the way she grades tests with the partial credit allowed me to score high on every test. My class was a hybrid class, so lectures on your own.

LISTEN! She is an extremely tough grader and gives little to no partial credit. She does not give very good feedback at all, and when she does, questions were not answered and more were added into the mix. When you have to add A thirty point curve to the final exam you know something went wrong. AVOID AT ALL COSTS! LISTEN TO THIS WEBSITE'S COMMENTS

Awful teacher. messed up multiple times while teaching and had to start all over and had to be corrected by students. seemed confused most of the time and didn't teach in an organized way. went through lectures fast and not very nice.

analysis.name: Melissa Foster
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 70 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Its an easy A Class and workload is pretty manageable for a Gen Ed. Its a Essay a Week and the directions are straight foward, and not too demanding. You learn alot and she clearly cares alot about the subject of Communication. My only real compliant is some of the stuff we learn is sort of Woke and it gets to a point after a while.

This class is supposed to be a easy 1 credit class, she makes it seem like a 4 credit class with the amount of lecturing and work she assigns us, she takes off points just so students do not get 100. showed her my project before submitting and she had nothing to say to improve it. Just not a good professor, thinks she owns the place and is rude.

I honestly loved this class. If you like writing and want a challenge, I'd take this class. If you're looking to do the bare minimum and get out, I would listen to all the other reviews and not take it. Dr. Foster as a teacher was great! Her lectures were interesting, she wasn't intimidating, and it was easy to set up appointments outside of class.

She treats us like we're in 3rd grade. like, we are in college, so why do you assign seats? She also is very rude and does not respond to questions when you email her. I emailed her about a question on an assignment and instead of getting my question answered, I got a rude response. I don't know how she has a teaching degree.

analysis.name: Naomi Fukumori
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 29 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Fukumori Sensei is really nice, and she offers a lot of extra credit, but it wasn't until the week before finals when she finally graded the FIRST paper. I went the entire semester not knowing my grade. The course is easy enough, but the lack of grading really soured the experience for me.

I agree with a previous review that she's a lenient grader; she was willing to give late credit for very past due assignments. She also takes a while to grade assignments but I wasn't bothered by it. There's a lot of reading, so it's very easy to fall behind. If you put in the time and effort, it's not hard to pass.
One of the best professors at the Ohio State. She knows her teaching material very well and cares about students' feelings. A really nice person.

The course had a lot of reading, but the writing assignments were pretty easy. There were only two actual essays, the rest being more opinions-based writings. Lectures can feel long, but she's very passionate about the material. She's also a pretty lenient grader, as long as you put in some effort.

Had her for Ancient Kyoto. She loves the material she teaches and it shows in her lectures. She does her best to make class interesting and enjoyable. She was the highlight to a very dull semester. I would happily recommend her to anyone.

analysis.name: Frank Stratman
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 30 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Professor Stratman is one of the best professors I have ever had. Respectful, Hilarious. Great Class. Clear materials. Gives feedback and willing to help. Would definitely take any of his classes.

Frank is great. Class time is the best thing about him; he fosters discussion and critical thinking from the get-go. Homework is super easy: quizzes and multiple-choice exams are direct from the textbook(s), discussion posts and papers are straightforward- just follow instructions. One downside to be aware of is he's not a fan of notetaking.
Flexible and understanding. Easy to get along with and very helpful.

Frank Stratman is such an amazing and knowledgeable person! I just really loved him so much and this class. You will learn so much from his classes. He is definitely one of the best professors at OSU and I think everyone should take at least one class with him. It's also a pretty easy class so you will do well, just get the textbook for the quizzes
Very fair and helpful. Assignments were very easy and it was easy to get a 100 on exams as well.

analysis.name: Han Gil Kim
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: This class was co taught by three prof, and all of them contributed positively to the learning experience. Dr. Kim's strengths were in clear explanation and thoughtful feedback. Both in and outside of class, he provided detailed explanations of supplementary and beyond class materials, and always took the time to answer questions thoroughly.

Professor Kim is a fun and engaging instructor. Even though the class discusses in depth topics, he brings many sources that help us understand the ideas clearly. He is also very supportive outside of class and shared helpful advice about career paths. Sometimes we even had class outside on a bench, which made the course memorable.

I really enjoyed this class! Lectures were fun bc Prof Kim is funny plus the information is interesting. Quizzes/tests were a bit hard to study for since I never know what to focus on so focus on everything :( & try not to miss class bc you'll miss info that u could be tested on. overall good class.

Dr. Kim's Elements of Korean Culture class was engaging and enriching. I learned so much about Korean history and culture. Though the workload seemed heavy at first, Dr. Kim was considerate with extensions and gave exceptional feedback on every essay, helping us improve our writing. A truly valuable course!

Professor Kim is a great instructor — he's informative, goes into more depth about complex grammar, and is proactive about answering questions. He's also really funny at times. However, his ACT classes are nerve-wracking. He isn't mean or anything, but he'll put you on the spot and doesn't really offer any help as you struggle to answer something.

analysis.name: Gabriel Gorre
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: All readings and lectures except midterm, a small group presentation, and participation (can be done through email and can be submitted at any point during the semester). Although he's really lenient with attendance, I would recommend sitting in as many lectures as possible because he makes everything really interesting and easy to understand.

This guy is the goat. Super fun class; attendance is graded, but he's very forgiving if you miss. One midterm and a group project are the only things you are graded on (and attendance). Lots of readings, but they can be fun, so it's not that bad. Super knowledgeable guy and fun to talk to. Overall, would recommend for poli sci majors.

He made our midterm exam available from March 2nd-6th, and sent an email saying that they will not be graded until mid-April or possibly later. The final exam is literally April 19th. How am I supposed to learn with no feedback?
Gorre is so nice and his class is super clearly instructed. You have a midterm and a final project and he is super direct about his expectations. He made the class pretty interesting.

analysis.name: Gregory Caldeira
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 27 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Greg was my professor for this class at the university of iowa. He was laid back and a good lecturer. I didn't find the class to be that demanding. I suppose if you want to get in law school it can be a challenge. But there is a glut of lawyers today as evidenced by the television commercials.

Worst polisci class I have taken at OSU. Like every1 says on here, multiple choice exams in which 1 word makes the difference if you get the ? right. Lectures, textbook, and online notes, are not helpful. attendance is a part of your grade (you will be board). If it was not for the curve, many students would end up with D's and F's. AVOID HIM!!!!!!

As an aspiring law student, his tests are multiple choice and extremely unpractical. Exams are on Carmen, which tends to glitch and mess up your answers. It gives you no room to make your case like an actual law exam. He uses this STUPID attendance taking software called Tophat that you'll waste $20  on.  Don't waste time. Find a better class.

If you aren't considering law school, or you don't have an interest in the Supreme Court, DO NOT TAKE THIS COURSE. That said, Caldeira is brilliant and highly regarded, look at his CV. He's extremely kind and helpful in office hours. Lectures are rough, he will lecture AT you for 80 minutes without stopping. Read every case and BRIEF THEM.

Professor Caldeira's lectures might be the cure to narcolepsy. He definitely knows his material but has no idea how to convey it. His notes are filled with acronyms that you will have no idea what they stand for when you go to prepare for his exams. He also uses terms that he does not go over and assumes you know what he's talking about.

analysis.name: Abraham Graber
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He assumed an honest assignment was AI and from there on was passive aggressive with grading & email responses. It's like he has a complex to make sure people who visibly care don't get a fair grade (like a weird power move & would intentionally mess with grades). I took this course out of genuine interest for the material and completely regret it.
Dr. Graber is extremely knowledgeable about the field of bioethics and had very interesting material to discuss and learn about.  Definitely one of my favorite courses I've taken.

The structure of the course is very clear, however, far too much work for a GE course. Written grading was subjective and did not reflect all points of view. Take the class if it is required, otherwise find something easier that won't take up too much of your time. Post-tests were written super confusingly that it made some questions illegible.

Overall, good professor. He's very thorough about AI, which may unintentionally penalize you if you're naturally a good writer, so keep a record of timestamps when writing. Also, no matter how close you are to getting the next letter grade up at the end of the semester, forget about it as he doesn't believe in rounding grades.

I loved his class! It seems like it will be a lot of work but I don't think it is, but I found the class very enjoyable! You usually have a quick reading, pre test (completion grade), discussion post (completion grade), and a post test. The pre-test and and post-test are the same questions and only like 8 questions. Great Professor and easy class!

analysis.name: Gyeongcheol Cho
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: This class is hard to understand and the homework and exams questions are tricky. He said that he knows the questions are tricky, there's only one letter difference between wrong and right answers, but he also said he will still include the tricky questions, because he think it is fair for everyone to truly understand the definitions.

Lectures are boring but attendance is mandatory. The class is not about actually understanding the concepts, but memorizing the professor's wording. Homework and test questions are convoluted and hard to understand. The professor also seems to not properly understand Canvas and TopHat and grades are not always accurately inputed at first.

The professor's lectures are boring and hard to understand. The homework is confusing and requires long and detailed handwriting. But he doesn't grade it; you have to rewrite the answers yourself. The exam accounts for a large proportion and the questions are strange. Your answers have to be based on his own explanations.

He was a super nice guy, but the lectures were so insanely boring and often hard to understand. I took AP stats in highschool so none of this material was new to me, but he words things super weird, and I was always confused. Slides are super helpful, but he leaves blanks that you have to fill in during class. Honestly, not sure how I got an A.

Lectures were incredibly dense and hard to understand-- he is a super nice guy but i hated absolutely everything about this class :(
Exams just tested if you understood his wording, not if you understood the actual class concepts

analysis.name: Aaron Gall
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Boxing for beginners is extremely fun and a good way to get rid of stress, Professor Aaron has also been around the world on side quests and is very knowledgeable about boxing and is awesome, there's like 6 quizzes and a exam where he gives you the answers to each question.

Aaron is an amazing coach/professor who is extremely caring and kind. His teaching methods are focused on teaching you the material of the course while also building you as a person and being there for you if necessary. He is there to help you succeed and work with you. This class is a great way to get exercise and learn the basics of Judo!
Great guy, very passionate, but I feel like out of the whole semester, I didn't really learn much
Awesome class, had a lot of fun, got exercise, and learned a lot about boxing. He wants you to do well, but skill isn't required for a good grade
Great guy, passionate about his classes and his students. Easy A if you show up and put in the work.

analysis.name: Ken Gordon
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 26 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I fully believe that Ken loves teaching and is insanely smart but he cannot explain anything on a basic, first time seeing the material level (which is what this class is). He makes you feel stupid for asking any questions and then doesn't answer them. The exams honestly weren't bad but the average homework grade was a D after spending hours on it.

I found his lectures and homework assignments to be very confusing. He sent many clarifications for homework as the instructions were hard to understand. Be ready to write long Matlab codes for homework which are strictly graded. When you ask a question in class he doesn't like to give a direct answer which is really frustrating.
Choose another instructor if you have other options. He makes you lose all your interests to your major, tough grader, boring classes, not posting the grades on Carmen, limit office hours!

He seems to love the material and is smart, but the pros end there. He has a very petty vibe/ mannerisms, which includes lecture, emails, answering questions, and through the tricky worded tests and HWs. I found this material to be difficult, and his condescending approach and single one hour office hours didn't help. Overall didn't enjoy the class

I prefer to study myself with aid of textbook and online free sources. He made this course harder than it should be. Heat transfer is no rocket science, I would prefer another lecturer for this course. This dog does not know how to respect students.

analysis.name: Amos Gilat
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 51 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Professor Gilat seems nice, but has no interest in this course, as he mentioned multiples times throughout the semester. Requires you to buy 2 of his awfully written textbooks, in which you will realize wont help you much for the 10+ hours of homework a week. Advice for incoming students: be prepared to learn this course on your own.

Made everything seem easy in class then I would struggle on homework and midterms, often not knowing what ideas to apply. The examples in class were helpful but it would have helped if he taught it in a way that we would know what concepts to apply depending on the problem statement and given information.

I wish he was more clear explaining what his mindset is in solving problems. He does nothing but do example problems in class, which I liked. I found this class is immensely difficult. Doesn't provide or let you make an equations sheet, so have fun memorizing tons of equations instead of actually studying. Just terrible.

Yes, he has an accent and some people think he's yelling at them, but it's just because he's passionate. He likes to answer your questions and will only lecture for 5 minutes each session then do practice problems which are pretty much what will be on the test. Great teacher, tests weren't bad if you do the homework and practice tests.

Gilat lectures well, his midterms were fair, his final was difficult, but he curved the class afterward. His office hours are helpful, and while he comes off as demeaning, he does not mean it. His homeworks are much harder than midterms, so it prepares you well for them. I think his bad rep is undeserved, people who show up and study do well.

analysis.name: Rebecca Harmon
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Starting off, Dr. Harmon is very kind and energetic. However, her teaching style is not the kind for anybody. Her ways just don't click; she uses a lot of filler words and goes on tangents. It doesn't seem like she speaks with a purpose, and she doesn't simplify the concepts AT ALL. Whenever I walk out of class, nobody has a clue on what's going on

Comp methods does not need to be as confusing as Dr. Harmon makes it seem. It would help so much if she explained the topics beforehand in simple terms and then expand upon them, but instead she just dives into the complicated content. I was expecting to learn Python (and was looking forward to it), but this course is mostly just math by hand.

Dr Harmon would lecture on a topic and not explain 90% of what was needed to understand what was being discussed. The requirements for homework and the final portfolio were also not very well explained where I had no idea what was needed in order to get a good grade in the class. She is extremely nice but she doesn't explain the topics very well.

Dr. Harmon is one of the nicest people I've met, but when it comes to having her for a class, watch out. I don't think I have learned one single thing from her this semester and I mean that. She makes stuff way more confusing than it has to be. Also exams are a time crunch and she doesn't help with questions.

Terrible lecturer despite how nice of a person she may seem to be. Does not seem to understand the material herself 90% of the time and confused the entire class. Gets derailed easily by questions asked during lecture and will spend the entire lecture trying to explain her answer (that she doesn't fully understand.)

analysis.name: Samantha Herrmann
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 49 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Unclear grading criteria (would always take points off for the silliest things), unnecessarily difficult tests and quizzes, not accessible outside of class, just no.

Very intelligent and easy to talk to! Great personality and sense of humor! This course involves a lot of time, especially for the project portion. There is so much information covered which made studying for exams difficult especially since there was no list provided as to what to even specifically study.  Definitely not an easy A.
Hard grader, not flexible or willing to work with you! You have to pay an extra fee to take the midterm and final at home even though it is an online class.
Her lectures are ridiculously long.
No help at all. Her tests are difficult. She doesn't give out study guides. She goes through lecture notes way too fast. Would not recommend for future students

analysis.name: Jinwoo Hwang
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I wish he taught more undergrad courses. His delivery is interesting, and he'll often include supporting context from current or historical events to motivate. He manages the material very well for a course with such broad footing.

Professor Hwang is very unintentionally (?) funny, and cares a lot about students. come up to him if you have issues. Only one homework this year, and 5 quizzes with two dropped. TAs were harsh graders.Waiting on final so expect B- to B+ for me.

Dr. Hwang is a great lecturer and his homework and exams were fair. The exam questions were similar to the homework, and he lets you have a one sided cheat sheet with anything you want. Make a good cheat sheet, redo the homework and you'll do well. I loved his jokes that came out of nowhere and how down to earth he was.

Professor Jinwoo is a clear public speaker. He teaches like a physicist, refreshingly. The content in Transport Phenomena is not difficult, compared to the companion MATSCEN3141 (Phase Transformations). There are no makeup examinations. There were about 100 students in one section, which strikes me as insane for a junior-level class.

analysis.name: Hun Lee
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I really enjoyed Hun's Leadership class! He has interesting lectures and he treats his students with respect and is genuinely curious about us as well.

Dr. Lee is a enthusiastic professor who cares about his students. Unfortunately, the praise ends there. The assignments are arbitrary. The class itself has no meaningful content. For an MBA course, I expected to be challenged and discuss complex topics, however the content was so meaningless, there was no intellectual discussions to be generated.

The expectations far exceed a 1.5CR course - there are several papers (ranging from 2-8 pages) plus a major research project and presentation. The feedback on assignments is very general and not at all helpful for improvement and understanding. The syllabus was not clear and asking questions didn't produce useful answers.

The expectations of this course far exceeds the 1.5CR hours.  The syllabus isn't detailed on the required assignments (2 minor papers, 2 major papers, and a recorded 15-20 minute presentation).  There were also required meetings outside of class with a randomly assigned group to discuss final project topics and progress.

This class was a 1.5 credit hour class however the work load was much more than expected out of a 1.5. The feedback on the grading was very general instead of giving direct feedback on the papers so the students would have an opportunity to learn to improve their grade. 
Nice professor though!

analysis.name: Haijun Su
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 29 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I thought some of my professors were bad-- Then I had Haijun Su. His lectures were awful, the recitations were unhelpful, and the grading policy was atrocious. There were 5 homeworks, that took me 5+ hours each, and each was 10% of my grade in the class. The other 50% of the grade came from the final exam (Which had only 5 questions). Good luck.

Genuinely the worst professor I've ever had at OSU. Lectures were a waste of time, he barely explains anything and just incoherently rambles the whole time. The homeworks take a long time to complete and there isn't much opportunity to improve your grade. Unfortunately, he is the only person that teaches this course, so you can't even avoid him.

Dr. Su's 3751 was easily one of the worst classes I've ever taken. The midterm was pretty easy but the final was noticeably harder. Grading criteria was not clear on the final, as well. TAs were largely unavailable. I wanted to do well, but the instruction team's poor teaching and lack of help told me otherwise. The lectures were worthless. AVOID!!

This class was very disappointing. The material wasn't hard, but the poor teaching made it much more difficult. Lectures were disorganized and unhelpful, and questions were often brushed off. The final exam was much harder than the midterm, and grading was unclear. TAs were rarely available or helpful. Overall, I wouldn't recommend this class.

Easily the worst professor in the MAE department. His lectures are a complete waste of time. Just watch the recordings on double speed. The content is not that hard but he teaches it so poorly. The midterm is pretty easy and was online. The final was in person and was not too hard but Haijun takes off a lot of points for no good reason.

analysis.name: Marshall Haddock
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: All Haddock does is yell at the students and gets furious when they cannot perform a piece the way he wants it before he tells us how he wants it - if we played it perfectly, he wouldn't  have a job. Soloists are brought to the first rehearsal when students are SIGHTREADING bc no music was given in advance. There is no professionalism in the class.

I see some students are saying that you just have to get used to Haddock and he's showing students what the real world of music is like and that is 100% absolutely not the case. Professional orchestra conductors are selected because they are not like Haddock and are great at what they do b/c  they encourage beautiful music respectfully and kindly.

analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 54 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Can be extremely biased and has favorites despite what all the other ratings say. Helpful to some but unhelpful to those who he doesn't look highly upon based on his own opinion. Exams are difficult and are usually harder than any practice exam. We had no test corrections but I heard the students now do so i'm not totally sure what that is like.

It is very easy to pass this class as long as you do something to stay up to date, either live lectures or the lecture preps are all super helpful. There is tedious homework assignments. Make sure to do the sample tests before each midterm/final because they are very similar to the real one. Also make sure you have a good group for team assignments

Literally the GOAT. He has in-person, online, and recorded lectures; the homework is relevant and important to your learning, and before all the midterms and finals he gave the last three semesters of those same tests. He wrote the program that the homework is done in, and has a built-in feature for just getting the answer and he is accessible too.

Jim posts lecture videos on canvas so I did not attend lecture, but the videos provided enough information to understand the material and get good grades on tests. Jim also allows test corrections to earn back half credit and practice exams that make getting a good grade in the class decently easy even though some of the material is difficult

Talamo makes the class extremely easy to follow along and understand. He also does a great job preparing students for midterms, giving plenty of resources and information well in advance. The only downside for 1172 is there is plenty of homework through Chimera. Still, 100/100 would recommend.

analysis.name: John Tengatenga
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Mandatory attendance, readings every night, two exams and a group project, and discussion posts before every class (2 a week). Professor is boring, hard to hear and understand, and has confusing grading criteria. My main issue, however, is his rudeness. Told me and a classmate he would kick us out of class because we talked once.

Easy class and easy A at the cost of your sanity. He is very quiet and has a thick accent, making it impossible to understand him. He is constantly 10 mins late to his own class. He takes points off but gives no feedback as to why. Two midterms, a group presentation, an OPED, and weekly discussions make up your grade. Don't expect much from him

he grades randomly based on how he feels, will take off one point on a project and not disclose any reasoning, readings aren't interesting nor are they important, midterm was easy and inapplicable to the readings, professor was hard to hear

analysis.name: Todd Schneider
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Todd clearly cares about his students and wants them to succeed. While the amount of work could rarely be overwhelming, he really teaches things that seem genuinely useful. Assignments were usually very fair, and if you studied or listened in class you should have no problem with them. Pay attention because he's actually a solid professor.

ISE 2400 was only offered in the evening but Todd made it worthwhile. Participating in the in-class activities almost always ensured a good grade in the exams. Todd is caring, patient, and entertaining, not to mention passionate about teaching. His real-life experiences translate well into a learning setting and the group projects are insightful.
I took ISE 2400 with Todd. Material can be dense but is usually easy to understand.

Professor Schneider was the perfect professor for this class. Very funny guy who enjoys what he teaches. The only downsides are the course time slot and how many assignments there are. He described the course as "a 4 credit hour class for 7 weeks" as opposed to a 2 credit hour course for 7 weeks. The work wasn't difficult, but there was a lot.

Todd is a very solid, funny professor. The content, to me, was actually really interesting, though I don't know if that is the general consensus. The course is pretty easy, but the two midterms and the finals were always a decent bit harder than the lectures and quizzes would make you think they'd be.

analysis.name: Timothy Jaquet
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Currently have him for ENR2501. A tough grader, full of himself, among other things. Likes to nitpick and take off points for every. little. thing. I'm not sure if he's harder on us because it's an online class, but I would stay away. Work isn't that bad, but the grading is harsh for no reason.

Ok lecturer, cares for material but can skip over details. Chatted after class few times, felt a bit high horse almost scoffed when presented ideas he deemed below him. Main issue: grading. Beware bad groups (randomly assigned)or projects feels like he doesn't care even with evidence before & after grading, felt stubborn yet unclear scoring/rubric.

Tim is a good professor but an absolutely horrible grader. He is very judgmental of the way students word their papers and will often deduct points for very small errors. When asking for help, he can be very unclear about what you need to do and often says things that are not helpful at all. He has a very high ego and has his favorites. Sus assess
I currently have him for ENR 2501. There are words to describe this man. And they're not good.
Great dude! Had his for ENR 2501. I'd recommend everyone take this class for an easy way to knock out the choice theme GE. He may have been my favorite professor of my semester

analysis.name: Kareem Usher
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Kareem's lectures weren't great but he was generally too vague. He has very few assignments and the majority of it is group work but seems to take points off just because he feels he can't give out all A's. Don't buy the textbook even if he makes you think you need to and beware: he won't mention the strict attendance policy until the sem. is over.

Though the class was taught primarily by GTAs, whenever Dr. Usher would lead lecture it was uninspired and often embarrassing. He has a lot of experience in the field of planning but struggles to use that experience to create lectures which engage the class and teach the course material.

Dr. Usher is a very good professor, and his lectures were really informative. He always tries his best to engage the rest of the students in discussion during the lecture. He is very educated on his topic. Assignments aren't difficult, but attendance is weighted very heavily for this course.

he is so kind, the class itself feels unorganized because there is him and two phd students teaching as well. I feel he is clearer and more informative than them and if you go to him with a question he can definitely answer
Definitely recommend Dr Usher is great!

analysis.name: Zach Usmani
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Great guy who cares more about content of work rather than if it was submitted on time or met all the correct criteria. Super nice.
Just. Great. He may care your grade more than yourself. Extremely lively lectures.
Zach is extremely helpful with assignments and is very informed on policy and social work. I would highly recommend his classes to anyone who is interested in policy and macro practice.
Great professor and fast at grading!

Zach really knows his stuff when it comes to policy and social work practice. I learned way more than I was expecting to in seminar. He is also very lenient with due dates and when grading he cares more about content than format.

analysis.name: Ulises Juan Zevallos-Aguilar
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: He yaps A LOT. He once told us that Red Bull gives you stomach cancer, though there's no scientific evidence to back that up. There've been so many instances in which he's gone on a tangent and I'm just thinking to myself "How did I get here?" Why are we yapping about Star Wars and Lord of the Rings during a class discussion about neoliberalism?

He's a nice guy, but my entire grade for the semester was based off 100 points. This was very frustrating as having 4 points docked from a midterm or final (which made up near 50% of your grade) dropped your grade significantly. Attend class or you'll be screwed. He offers some extra credit, but not enough assignments with how little there are.

While the class ended up being easy to get a good grade, getting there was tough. He was not at all clear during lectures, and we never even talked about the actual poetry that the class was about, just beat around the bush. But that doesn't mean he didn't expect you to know it anyway. I'd say about 1/2 of each class was a total waste of time.

He was a pretty bad professor. He went on tangents in every class and wouldn't let anyone talk. He has a certain answer in mind when he asks a question and any other answer is just flat out wrong. Most of the class is pointless and you probably don't need to go but, he grades on participation and attendance, so that sucks. DON'T TAKE HIM.

He's a good guy, but I'm not sure what the point of the class was. We did e-journals for every class, a student presented on the reading, and then we had a class discussion. I'm not really sure what he did other than hand out a 93% to everyone. He's a nice guy and can be funny, but this class was a little frustrating.

analysis.name: Hugh Urban
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 49 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Great professor! 4 papers (grades super easy), and one final group project (if you show up and present you get full credit). Mandatory attendance, but very light work class for a 4000 level course. Definitely take him.
Definitely one of the rock star professors at OSU. His class will be interesting and worthwhile.

The Hughru is a GREAT professor. He is very passionate about the subject matter and likes to have a good time while teaching. He uses a lot videos and class discussion to keep you interested. He is very skilled at rewording your answer to make you sound like a genius.
Showing up to class is automatically 30% of your grade. He is a great professor, his class is interesting and well taught, and the material is fairly easy.

Prof. Urban deals very well with the touchy subject of religion, and keeps his own opinions to himself pretty well. He really lets the students discuss the issues than touting his own opinion and he does very well at moderating the discussion, too. Attend lecture & recitation and take notes, and you shouldn't have problems with papers and tests.

analysis.name: Jacob Urick
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Guy dumped water all over my girlfriend right in front of me. Super weird.
TOP lad.... stood at the front of class with a super soaker and absolutely drenched people for getting questions wrong... righteous stuff

He once walked into office hours with a 5-gallon container of purified water slung over his shoulder, and proceeded to dump its contents onto a nearby female student. Thereafter Jacob immediately began to boisterously celebrate, punting the empty gallon at the ceiling and beckoning observers to chant as he completed a victory lap around the room.

Honestly just not a good guy. Demeaning with his vocabulary. Needed a bit of extra help and my questions were deflected and left unanswered. He is borderline unreachable and would cancel class at the last possible minute, rumored to be due to impromptu beach trips. Never felt so talked down to by an individual

This Guy went out of his way for kids to do bad in his class.  He would never answer any questions in class and was not a good guy overall.  Really wish I had the chance to leave the class when I did.

analysis.name: Upul Ranaweera
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Had him for Chem 1210. Everything was ok up until he served out the final exam that had 10 extra multiple choice questions and double the amount of short answer questions (when compared to the normal exams) and still gave us the same amount of time as if it were a normal exam.
an amazing professor just make sure to go to class

I took 1220 and 2540 with him. Chemistry is hard but he made it easier to understand. He has a good sense of humor and definitely really cares about his students. He might grade hard, but he will always explain why. His class is difficult, but he is always willing to answer questions during lab and explain things at office hours.

analysis.name: Ulrich Gerlach
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I took a class in General Relativity from Professor Gerlach in 1973. He set me on a course of learning Physics, in which I previously had no interest. He was the biggest influence on my way of thinking ever.  I have spent 40 years pondering and reviewing the things he taught me. I am very grateful to Professor Gerlach.

Professor Gerlach is such a great lecturer and mentor to general relativity. He apperantly has vast knowldege on mathematics and physics. He is trying to help you build solid pictures on physics. You may think the course is lack of examples. But he is super available and willing to help everyone! Talk with him outside of the classroom. Respected!

He is a very nice and great professor, and he knows how to teach. He really wants to let you understand. He encourages you to ask questions in the class and he will even give you a penny for finding his mistake. 
Accessible outside the classroom, and very knowledgeable, will answer your questions in math, physics and engineering.
Very good teacher.  I learned a lot from him. He makes sure you understand and if he sees the class does not understand a topic well he would revisit it.  His way of teaching is really good also.
Outstanding professor, who used to give you a penny if you caught him making a typo/mistake on the whiteboard. Kept you alert!

analysis.name: Erin Upchurch
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Communication was horrible. I have an assignment from March 4th that hasn't been graded yet and it's almost May... The course has assignments that build on each other (parts 1-4) but you will absolutely not get any grade or feedback by the time the next part is due. When multiple students complained, we all got 20/20 with no feedback the next day..

Didn't learn anything from her. Online class, she was extremely late grading assignments, didn't respond to emails and would accept no late assignments, although she gave herself permission to be late and provided no feedback on assignments. Wasn't engaged in the course at all. Avoid taking courses from this instructor.

Erin is very transparent about her expectations and background as a social worker. The class emphasizes diversity and advocacy, and she really brought her experiences to the table. Participation was key. Erin really connected us to the material and the reality of the social work profession. Always respectful and honest.

My favorite professor at OSU! She has so much knowledge beyond what she's teaching. Class is easy, with lots of open conversations and guest speakers. She clearly cares about the mental health of her students. Was not a fast grader, but was an easy grader. I highly recommend taking a class with her!
Ridiculous turnaround time on grades. 4-6 weeks to get grades back on papers. No responses to emails. Vague instructions with no clarification.
